etymology.description: semantic 竹(竹) (zhú, "bamboo") + phonetic 即(即) (jí)
etymology.interpretation: 節(Jié, "festival; node; joint; segment") combines 竹(竹) (zhú, "bamboo") with 即(即) (jí) as a phonetic complement. Bamboo represents the semantic aspect due to its segmented structure, reminiscent of nodes or joints. The phonetic 即(即) complements this by providing the sound, without contributing a semantic layer, leading to the meanings related to segmentation, such as 'node', 'joint', or 'segment', and through metaphorical extension, 'festival' as a demarcated time.
etymology.simplification: 节(節) simplifies 節 by removing the 竹(竹) (zhú, "bamboo") radical, focusing solely on the phonetic 即(即) (jí) with a visually simpler form.

examples.chinese: 春节是中国最重要的节日(春節是中國最重要的節日)。
examples.pinyin: Chūnjié shì Zhōngguó zuì zhòngyào de jiérì.
examples.english: Spring Festival is the most important festival in China.

examples.chinese: 这根竹子的每个节都很明显(這根竹子的每個節都很明顯)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè gēn zhúzi de měi gè jié dōu hěn míngxiǎn.
examples.english: Each joint on this bamboo stalk is very distinct.

examples.chinese: 我们公司最近完成了一个项目的第一个阶段(我們公司最近完成了一個項目的第一個階段)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen gōngsī zuìjìn wánchéngle yīgè xiàngmù de dì yī gè jiēduàn.
examples.english: Our company recently completed the first segment of a project.
